compiled_state.title: Add integrated observability: context tracking, cost analytics, and agent telemetry
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Add an integrated observability layer to DSO that tracks context usage, cost, skill/agent performance, and review quality — with log rotation to prevent runaway disk usage.

## Reference: awesome-claude-code Ecosystem Gap

The awesome-claude-code list (36k stars) identifies observability as the clearest gap in the Claude Code ecosystem. Existing tools (claude-devtools, ccflare) are external read-only viewers. DSO has the opportunity to build observability directly into its orchestration layer, where the data is richest.

## What to Track

### 1. Context Usage Telemetry
- Context window utilization per phase (brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan, sprint batch, review, commit)
- Context at compaction trigger points (when did 70% threshold hit?)
- Sub-agent context consumption per dispatch (how much of the 200k window did each sub-agent use?)
- Compaction events: voluntary vs. involuntary, what was lost

### 2. Cost Per Phase
- Token usage (input + output) per skill invocation
- Token usage per sub-agent dispatch
- Aggregated cost per story, per epic, per sprint
- Model tier usage breakdown (haiku vs. sonnet vs. opus tokens)
- Cost efficiency: tokens per task completed, tokens per line of code changed

### 3. Skill and Sub-Agent Telemetry
- Invocation count per skill
- Success/failure rate per skill
- Average duration per skill
- Sub-agent dispatch count, success rate, and checkpoint completion rate (1/6 through 6/6)
- Model escalation frequency (how often does haiku→sonnet→opus escalation occur?)
- Dispatch failure rate and recovery method used

### 4. Review Quality Metrics
- Review score averages per dimension (correctness, verification, hygiene, design, maintainability)
- Review tier distribution (light/standard/deep percentages)
- Overlay activation frequency (security, performance)
- Autonomous resolution success rate and attempt distribution
- False positive rate (findings dismissed or downgraded)

### 5. Test and Validation Metrics
- Test pass/fail rate per run
- Test gate block frequency
- Validation suite duration trends
- RED test lifecycle (time from RED marker added to marker removed)

### 6. Ticket System Metrics
- Tickets created/closed per session
- Average time from open to closed per ticket type
- Bug discovery rate (bugs found per sprint)
- Epic completion velocity (stories/day)

## Storage and Rotation

### Log Format
- Structured JSON lines (.jsonl) — one event per line, each with timestamp, event_type, and payload
- Stored at .claude/telemetry/ (or configurable via dso-config.conf telemetry.dir)
- Separate files per category: context.jsonl, cost.jsonl, agents.jsonl, reviews.jsonl, tests.jsonl, tickets.jsonl

### Rotation and Compaction
- **Size-based rotation** — rotate when a log file exceeds configurable max size (default 5MB)
- **Time-based rotation** — rotate daily or weekly (configurable)
- **Retention policy** — keep N rotated files (default 10), delete oldest
- **Compaction** — when rotating, compact the old file into a summary record (totals, averages, min/max per metric) before archiving. Raw events are discarded; summary is appended to a persistent summaries.jsonl
- **Disk budget** — configurable max total telemetry size (default 50MB). If exceeded, oldest rotated files are pruned first, then summaries are compacted further.
- **.gitignore** — telemetry directory must be gitignored (contains session-specific data, not project artifacts)

### Configuration
- telemetry.enabled=true/false (default true)
- telemetry.dir=.claude/telemetry
- telemetry.max_file_size_mb=5
- telemetry.rotation=daily
- telemetry.retention_count=10
- telemetry.max_total_size_mb=50
- telemetry.categories=context,cost,agents,reviews,tests,tickets (comma-separated, all by default)

## Reporting

### /dso:retro Integration
- The retro skill should consume telemetry data as a primary input alongside its existing git/ticket analysis
- Retro should surface: cost trends, context efficiency, agent success rates, review score trends, test health

### On-Demand Dashboard
- A /dso:telemetry or /dso:stats skill that summarizes current session and historical telemetry
- Output: structured summary with sparkline-style trends where applicable

### Sprint Summary
- /dso:end session close should include a telemetry summary: total tokens used, cost estimate, sub-agents dispatched, review scores, tests run

comments.body:
## Reference: wshobson/agents PluginEval Monte Carlo Simulation

wshobson/agents (33k stars) implements PluginEval — a three-layer evaluation engine that statistically validates whether skills and agents actually trigger correctly at runtime:

| Layer | Speed | Cost | What It Measures |
|-------|-------|------|-----------------|
| Static analysis | <2s | Free | Frontmatter, orchestration wiring, progressive disclosure, token efficiency, structural completeness |
| LLM judge (Haiku + Sonnet concurrent) | ~30s | 4 calls | Triggering accuracy, orchestration fitness, output quality, scope calibration |
| Monte Carlo simulation | 2-6 min | 50-100 calls | Activation rate, output consistency, failure rate, token efficiency with Wilson/Bootstrap/Clopper-Pearson confidence intervals |

10 quality dimensions with explicit weights: triggering accuracy (25%), orchestration fitness (20%), output quality (15%), scope calibration (12%), progressive disclosure (10%), token efficiency (6%), robustness (5%), structural completeness (3%), code template quality (2%), ecosystem coherence (2%).

Badge tiers: Platinum ≥90, Gold ≥80, Silver ≥70, Bronze ≥60. CI integration: exits with code 1 below threshold.

DSO's observability layer should incorporate similar meta-quality measurement — not just tracking what agents do, but statistically validating that our skills and agents trigger correctly, produce consistent outputs, and operate within their token budgets. This goes beyond runtime telemetry into proactive reliability measurement.
comments.author: Claude
